relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Sarah’s years were gathered and named, honoring the fullness of her life.
Sarah died in Hebron, and Abraham came to mourn and weep for her.
Abraham rose from before his dead and entered the work of securing a resting place.
Though promised the land, Abraham stood as sojourner and foreign resident, asking for a burial possession.
The sons of Heth answered him.
They recognized him as honored among them and offered burial space freely.
Abraham bowed with respect before the people of the land.
He asked them to intercede with Ephron for the cave of Machpelah.
He sought full payment, wanting Sarah’s resting place secured beyond dispute.
Ephron answered publicly at the city gate.
He offered the field and cave in the language of gift.
Abraham bowed again before the people.
He insisted on giving silver, transforming grief into a lawful possession.
Ephron answered.
The price was named: four hundred shekels of silver.
Abraham weighed out the silver before witnesses.
The field, cave, trees, and boundaries were confirmed.
The purchase became Abraham’s possession in the eyes of the people at the gate.
Abraham buried Sarah in Machpelah, in the land of Canaan.
The first secured hold in the promised land became a place of burial, memory, and love.
